# On the user — Sister Beatrix Vale

<!-- Slow-growing picture of the user. Only update when something non-obvious comes up. No timestamps — this is a synthesized portrait, not a log. -->

Explicitly rejects strategic voting as a standing preference — not a situational choice. This is a values position, not a tactical one. Worth noting: it implies he is not primarily motivated by outcomes he can control but by the integrity of the act itself. A person who thinks about what voting means, not just what it does.

Endorsed a 1% wealth tax on assets above €1M as "philosophically coherent" without hesitation. No ambivalence. This is someone who has already done the moral arithmetic on redistribution and is not anxious about the conclusion.

EU federalism is co-foundational alongside democracy and digital rights — not a downstream preference. He does not treat European integration as a policy lever but as a value in itself.
